name: runpane
description: Thin installer and local control CLI for Pane
packageInstallPolicy: The packages must not download, install, or configure Pane during package installation.
Work starts only when a user runs `runpane ...`.

codex.title: Codex
codex.description: Open a Codex terminal tab and allow the initial input to drive the agent.

claude.title: Claude Code
claude.description: Open a Claude Code terminal tab and allow the initial input to drive the agent.

commands.name: help
commands.summary: Show help for runpane or a specific command.

commands.name: setup
commands.summary: Open the guided setup wizard for install, remote host setup, update, and diagnostics.

commands.summary: Install Pane on this machine or configure this machine as a remote daemon host.

commands.summary: Update the Pane desktop app using the same artifact path as install client.

wrapper.name: --version
wrapper.description: Pane release to install or inspect.

wrapper.name: --download-dir
wrapper.description: Directory for downloaded Pane release artifacts.

wrapper.name: --pane-path

wrapper.description: Print the plan without downloading or installing.

localValue.name: --pane-dir
localValue.description: Connect to a Pane daemon using this Pane data directory.

localValue.name: --repo
localValue.description: Repository selector: active, id, exact path, or saved repository name.

localValue.name: --panel
localValue.description: Tool panel id to inspect or control.

localValue.name: --path
localValue.description: Existing git repository path to register with Pane.

localValue.name: --name
localValue.description: Name for the registered repository or created pane/session.

localValue.name: --worktree-name
localValue.description: Worktree name to request. Defaults to --name.

localValue.name: --base-branch
localValue.description: Base branch for the created worktree.

localValue.name: --agent
localValue.description: Built-in agent terminal template to open.

localValue.name: --tool-command
localValue.description: Custom terminal command to run instead of a built-in agent.

localValue.name: --title
localValue.description: Terminal tab title. Defaults to the selected agent title or Terminal.

localValue.name: --initial-input
localValue.description: Text to send to the terminal after the command is ready. --prompt is an alias.

localValue.name: --initial-input-file
localValue.description: Read initial input from a file or stdin.

localValue.name: --from-json
localValue.description: Read a full panes.create request JSON payload from a file or stdin.

localValue.name: --timeout-ms
localValue.description: Maximum time to wait for each pane creation job.

localValue.name: --limit
localValue.description: Maximum number of output records to read.

localValue.name: --text
localValue.description: Text bytes to send to a terminal panel.

localValue.name: --input-file
localValue.description: Read panel input from a file or stdin.

default: Run "runpane agent-context" when an agent needs Pane command context.
Run "runpane help panes create" for pane orchestration options.
install: pnpm dlx runpane@latest install daemon --prefer-tunnel ssh --label "VM"
setup: Opens the guided setup for desktop install, remote host setup, update, and diagnostics.
update: Updates Pane using the same artifact selection as "runpane install client".
repos list: Lists repositories saved in the running Pane app.
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
repos add: Registers an existing git repository with the running Pane app.
--name <name>                  Saved repository name; defaults to the directory name
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
--dry-run                      Validate and preview without adding the repo
panes list: Lists Pane sessions. Pass --repo to limit results to one saved repository.
--repo <selector>              active, id, exact path, or saved repository name
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panes create: Creates Pane sessions in a saved repository and opens a terminal-backed tool tab.
--repo <selector>              active, id, exact path, or saved repository name
--initial-input <text>         Text sent after the command is ready
--initial-input-file <path|->  Read initial input from a file or stdin
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
--dry-run                      Validate and preview without creating panes
panels list: Lists tool panels in a Pane session.
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panels output: Reads recent terminal output records from a panel.
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panels input: Sends exact input bytes to a terminal panel. Include a newline in the input when you mean Enter.
--input-file <path|->          Read input from a file or stdin
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
agent-context: Prints Pane command context for coding agents without requiring a running Pane app.
--command <command>            Print the full definition for one runpane command

maintainerRules: Treat `contracts/runpane/contract.json` as the source of truth for both wrapper packages and generated docs.
Every command, flag, platform default, artifact-selection rule, and attribution rule change must be reflected in the contract.
The npm and PyPI wrappers must expose the same command behavior unless the contract explicitly documents a package-manager-specific difference.
Root `README.md` and package READMEs should lead with one guided quick-start command. Explicit commands, package-manager variants, and flags belong in an Advanced section.
Release version bumps must keep root `package.json`, `packages/runpane`, and `packages/runpane-py` versions in sync. Run `pnpm run check:runpane-package-versions` before release.
`pnpm run test:runpane-contract` must pass before changing wrapper command parsing, help output, platform defaults, release asset selection, or generated contract artifacts.
Token-based npm or PyPI publishing is a temporary fallback. Prefer trusted publishing once the package names are reserved and trusted publishers are configured.
recommendedQuickStarts: python -m pip install runpane && python -m runpane setup
npmCommands: pnpm dlx runpane@latest install daemon --label "My Server"
npm i -g runpane && runpane install daemon --label "My Server"
pnpm add -g runpane && runpane install daemon --label "My Server"
yarn dlx runpane@latest install daemon --label "My Server"
pythonCommands: pipx run runpane install daemon --label "My Server"
packageManagerNotes: Use `pnpm dlx` for one-shot pnpm execution and `pnpm add -g` for persistent CLI installation.
Do not document `pnpm install runpane` as the public CLI install path.
commandUsages: runpane panes create --repo active --name issue-252 --agent codex --prompt "Kick off the discussion skill for issue 252" --yes
commandDescriptions: `runpane` with no arguments and `runpane setup` open an interactive wizard when stdin and stdout are TTYs. In non-interactive shells or CI, both forms must print help and exit successfully instead of waiting for input.
`runpane install` is an alias for `runpane install client`.
`runpane install client` downloads the selected Pane desktop artifact and installs, opens, or launches it for the current platform.
`runpane install daemon` downloads or installs Pane, resolves a stable Pane executable path, and spawns `<pane executable> --remote-setup <forwarded remote setup args>`.
The wrapper must stream Pane stdout/stderr without reformatting because `pane --remote-setup` prints the one-time `pane-remote://...` connection code.
`runpane update` uses the same release resolution and installer path as `install client`.
`runpane version` prints the wrapper package version, the installed Pane version when detectable, and the latest GitHub release version when reachable.
`runpane doctor` checks platform support, release metadata reachability, download URL selection, installed Pane detection, and remote-daemon hints.
`runpane agent-context` prints a brief, token-efficient command schema for coding agents without connecting to the Pane daemon.
`runpane agent-context --command "panes create"` prints the detailed definition for one command. Add `--json` for machine-readable output.
`runpane repos list` connects to the running local Pane daemon and prints saved repository records.
`runpane repos add` registers an existing git repository with the running local Pane daemon. It does not create directories or initialize git repositories by default.
`runpane panes list` lists Pane sessions, optionally scoped to one saved repository.

`runpane panes create` connects to the running local Pane daemon, resolves the requested repository, creates Pane sessions, opens terminal-backed tool tabs, and optionally sends initial input to the started tool.
`runpane panels list` lists tool panels inside one Pane session.
`runpane panels output` reads recent terminal output records from one panel.
`runpane panels input` sends exact input bytes to one terminal panel. Include a newline in the input when the agent means Enter.
`runpane panes create --prompt` is an alias for `--initial-input`; request JSON and daemon payloads should use the canonical `initialInput` field.

When running from WSL while Pane is installed on Windows, the Linux wrapper may look for a missing `/tmp/pane-daemon.../daemon.sock`. In that case invoke the Windows wrapper through PowerShell, for example `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane repos list --json'`.
docs.wrapperFlagNote: The top-level `runpane --version` form prints the wrapper version. The install subcommand form `runpane install --version vX.Y.Z` selects a Pane release.
docs.localControlFlagNote: `runpane repos list`, `runpane panes list`, `runpane panes create`, and `runpane panels ...` commands use the local framed daemon socket/pipe for a running Pane app. `--pane-dir` points the wrapper at a non-default Pane data directory, such as `PANE_DIR=~/.pane_test` in development. `runpane agent-context` is local/offline and can be used before Pane is running. From WSL, if the user runs Windows Pane, call the Windows wrapper through `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane ...'` so the command can reach the Windows named-pipe daemon.
docs.daemonFlagNote: Unknown daemon flags should be forwarded rather than dropped so newer Pane versions can extend `--remote-setup` without requiring an immediate wrapper release. Unknown flags for non-daemon commands should fail clearly.
downloadAttribution: The npm package uses `source=npm` for all npm-registry consumers, including `npx`, `pnpm dlx`, `yarn dlx`, `bunx`, and global npm/pnpm installs.
The PyPI package uses `source=pip` for all Python consumers, including pip, pipx, uvx, and `python -m runpane`.
If the website route cannot satisfy the download, wrappers may fall back to the matching GitHub release asset and print a warning that website attribution may be incomplete for that run.
publishingCredentials: Local implementation, build, and dry-run validation do not need npm or PyPI API tokens.
Release publishing should prefer npm Trusted Publishing and PyPI Trusted Publishing from GitHub Actions.

Fallback `NPM_TOKEN` or `PYPI_API_TOKEN` credentials may be used for first package reservation or manual publication only. They must be supplied through local environment variables or GitHub Actions secrets, never committed, and revoked or rotated after use.

pipHelpIncludes: python -m pip install runpane && python -m runpane setup

brief.title: Pane agent context
brief.summary: Pane lets a developer manage repositories and user-visible panes. Agents can use runpane to list/add Pane repositories and create panes with terminal-backed tools.
rules: Start with `runpane repos list --json` to find the saved repository when unsure.
If WSL cannot find `/tmp/pane-daemon.../daemon.sock`, the user may be running Windows Pane; try `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane repos list --json'`.
If the repository exists on disk but is not saved in Pane, use `runpane repos add --path <repo> --yes --json` before creating panes.
Use `runpane panes create` to create a Pane session and open a built-in agent or custom terminal command.
Use `runpane panels list`, `runpane panels output`, and `runpane panels input` when you need to inspect or drive an existing Pane terminal.
Use `runpane agent-context --command <command>` for detailed command definitions only when needed.

help.name: help
help.summary: Show help for runpane or a specific command.
help.details: Use this when you need human-readable usage text for a command.

arguments.name: command
arguments.description: Optional command topic such as "panes create".

notes: Help text is generated from the runpane contract.

setup.name: setup
setup.summary: Open the guided setup wizard for install, remote host setup, update, and diagnostics.
setup.details: Use this for a human-driven Pane setup flow, not for unattended agent orchestration.
notes: In non-interactive shells, setup prints help and exits successfully.

install.name: install
install.summary: Install Pane on this machine or configure this machine as a remote daemon host.
install.details: Installs or launches Pane release artifacts at command runtime. `install daemon` forwards remote setup flags to Pane.

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Install the desktop client or configure a remote daemon host.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Pane release to install.

arguments.name: --format
arguments.description: Release artifact format.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip interactive prompts where possible.

notes: Package install itself is inert; work begins only when runpane is executed.

update.name: update
update.summary: Update the Pane desktop app using the same artifact path as install client.
update.details: Resolves and installs the selected Pane client release.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Pane release to update to.

arguments.name: --format
arguments.description: Release artifact format.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print the update plan without downloading.

notes: Equivalent artifact selection to `runpane install client`.

version.name: version
version.summary: Print the runpane wrapper version and installed Pane version when detectable.
version.details: Use this for diagnostics or to confirm which wrapper is available.

arguments.name: --pane-path
arguments.description: Inspect a specific Pane executable.

doctor.name: doctor
doctor.summary: Run platform, release, installed Pane, and remote setup diagnostics.
doctor.details: Use this when runpane install/update behavior needs environment diagnostics.

arguments.name: --pane-path
arguments.description: Use an existing Pane executable for diagnostics.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Print extra diagnostics.

agent-context.name: agent-context
agent-context.summary: Print token-efficient Pane command context for coding agents.
agent-context.details: Use this first when an agent needs to discover Pane primitives. It is local/offline and does not require a running Pane app.

arguments.name: --command
arguments.description: Print the detailed definition for one command, for example "panes create".

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

notes: Default output is brief so AGENTS.md can point here without bloating context.

repos list.name: repos list
repos list.summary: List repositories saved in the running Pane app.
repos list.details: Use this to find the right Pane-managed repository before creating panes. If the repo is missing, use `repos add` first.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable repository records.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Requires a running Pane app or daemon for the selected Pane data directory.

If this fails from WSL with a missing `/tmp/pane-daemon.../daemon.sock`, the user may be running Windows Pane. Retry via `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane repos list --json'`.

repos add.name: repos add
repos add.summary: Register an existing git repository with the running Pane app.
repos add.details: Use this when the repository exists on disk but is not saved in Pane yet. It validates the path as a git repository.

arguments.name: --path
arguments.description: Existing git repository path to register with Pane.

arguments.name: --name
arguments.description: Saved repository name; defaults to the directory name.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for mutating commands.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Validate and preview without adding the repo.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: It does not create directories or initialize git repositories by default.
For a Windows Pane app managing WSL repositories, prefer a saved WSL repo from `repos list` when possible. Adding a brand-new WSL repo from Windows may require WSL-aware path handling.

panes list.name: panes list
panes list.summary: List Pane sessions, optionally scoped to a saved repository.
panes list.details: Use this after selecting a repository to find existing Pane sessions and their ids before inspecting panels.

arguments.name: --repo
arguments.description: Optional repository selector: active, id, exact path, or saved repository name.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Without --repo, this lists sessions across saved Pane repositories.

panes create.name: panes create
panes create.summary: Create one or more Pane sessions in a saved repository and open a terminal-backed tool tab.
panes create.details: Use this to set up user-visible panes for work. Select a saved repository, choose a built-in agent or custom terminal command, and optionally send initial input after the tool starts.

arguments.name: --repo
arguments.description: Repository selector: active, id, exact path, or saved repository name.

arguments.name: --name
arguments.description: Pane/session name.

arguments.name: --agent
arguments.description: Built-in agent terminal template to open.

arguments.name: --tool-command
arguments.description: Custom terminal command to run instead of a built-in agent.

arguments.name: --prompt
arguments.description: Alias for --initial-input; sends text after the command is ready.

arguments.name: --initial-input-file
arguments.description: Read initial input from a file or stdin.

arguments.name: --from-json
arguments.description: Read a full panes.create request JSON payload.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for mutating commands.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

examples: runpane panes create --repo active --name issue-257 --agent codex --prompt "Plan this issue" --yes
notes: At least one of --agent or --tool-command is required unless --from-json is used.
The built-in agent templates come from the runpane contract; custom terminal commands can pass agent-specific flags when requested by the user.

From WSL with Windows Pane, invoke through PowerShell and select the saved WSL repo by name or id, for example `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane panes create --repo "WSL Pane" --name issue-123 --agent codex --prompt "Plan this issue" --yes --json'`.

panels list.name: panels list
panels list.summary: List tool panels inside a Pane session.
panels list.details: Use this to find the terminal panel id to inspect or control after creating or selecting a Pane session.

arguments.name: --pane
arguments.description: Pane/session id.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: The ids returned here are stable inputs for `panels output` and `panels input`.

panels output.name: panels output
panels output.summary: Read recent terminal output from a panel.
panels output.details: Use this to inspect what is currently visible or recently emitted by a terminal-backed panel.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Tool panel id.

arguments.name: --limit
arguments.description: Maximum output records to read.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Default text output concatenates stdout/stderr-like records. Use --json when an agent needs timestamps and record types.

panels input.name: panels input
panels input.summary: Send input bytes to a terminal panel.
panels input.details: Use this to answer prompts or continue an agent inside an existing Pane terminal panel.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Terminal panel id.

arguments.name: --text
arguments.description: Text bytes to send.

arguments.name: --input-file
arguments.description: Read input from a file or stdin.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for this mutating command.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Input is sent exactly as provided. Include `\n` or `\r` when the terminal should receive Enter.
Runpane records action metadata and errors for observability, but should not log full input text by default.

managedBlock: The developer is using Pane for this repository. Pane can manage saved repositories and create user-visible panes with terminal-backed tools for planning, discussion, and implementation work.
Use `runpane agent-context` for a brief Pane command schema. Use `runpane agent-context --command "panes create"` or another command name for the detailed schema only when needed.

WSL note: if `runpane repos list` cannot find `/tmp/pane-daemon.../daemon.sock`, Pane may be running on Windows. Try `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane repos list --json'`, then create panes through the same PowerShell form using the saved WSL repo name or id.
